info.title: Vijil Console API (Combined)
info.description: Combined OpenAPI specification for all vijil-console microservices.

post.description:
Authenticate with email + password, sets JWT token in httpOnly cookie.

Args:
    login_request: Login credentials (email and password)
    response: FastAPI response object to set cookies
    user_manager: FastAPI Users user manager

Returns:
    JWT token response with access_token and token_type (token also set in httpOnly cookie)

Raises:
    HTTPException: If authentication fails

schema.title: Response Jwt Login V1 Auth Jwt Login Post

post.description:
Logout user by clearing the authentication cookie.

Args:
    response: FastAPI response object to clear cookies

Returns:
    Success message

schema.title: Response Jwt Logout V1 Auth Jwt Logout Post

get.description:
Get the currently authenticated user's information.

Args:
    current_user: Current authenticated user (from JWT claims)

Returns:
    Current user information

post.description:
Refresh JWT token.

Accepts a valid or expired JWT token and returns a new token with
fresh expiration. The token can be provided in a cookie or Authorization header.

Args:
    request: FastAPI request object
    response: FastAPI response object to set cookies
    user_manager: FastAPI Users user manager
    session: Database session

Returns:
    JWT token response with access_token and token_type (also sets cookie)

Raises:
    HTTPException: If token is invalid or user not found

schema.title: Response Jwt Refresh V1 Auth Jwt Refresh Post

post.description:
Change the current user's password.

Verifies the current password, updates to the new password,
clears the password_change_required flag, and returns a fresh JWT.

Args:
    request: Change password request with current and new passwords
    response: FastAPI response object to set cookies
    claims: JWT claims from middleware
    user_manager: FastAPI Users user manager
    session: Database session

Returns:
    JWT token response with access_token and token_type

Raises:
    UnauthenticatedException: If current password is invalid
    BadRequestException: If new password is same as current or invalid

schema.title: Response Change Password V1 Auth Change Password Post

post.description:
Create a new user.

Requires team:write_user permission for the specified team (team owners and admins) or
team:write_user_all permission (super admin).

get.description:
Get a user by query parameters.

Currently supports:
- email: Get user by email address

Additional query parameters can be added in the future.

parameters.name: email

schema.description: Email address of the user to retrieve
schema.title: Email

parameters.description: Email address of the user to retrieve

get.description:
Get the current authenticated user's information.

Returns the user information for the authenticated user based on JWT claims.

Get the current user's team memberships.

Returns all teams the authenticated user is a member of, along with
their role, name, and description.

schema.title: Response Get My Teams V1 Users Me Teams Get

get.description: Get a user by ID.

post.description:
Create a new team.

Requires team:create_all permission (super admin only).

Invite a user to a team by email.

Requires teams:invite permission (team owners and admins) or
team:invite_all permission (super admin).

Accept a team invitation.

Only the target user (the one who was invited) can accept their invitation.
The user_id and email come from the authenticated JWT token.

get.description:
List all users in a team.

Requires team:read permission (all team members) or
team:read_all permission (super admin).

schema.title: Response Get Team Users V1 Teams  Team Id  Users Get

get.description:
Get a team by ID.

Requires team:read permission (all team members) or
team:read_all permission (super admin).

Note: This route must come after more specific routes like /{team_id}/users
to avoid route conflicts.

post.description:
Create a new agent configuration.

This endpoint accepts AgentConfigRequest format from the frontend and transforms it
to the internal AgentCreate format. The agent_url field is preserved.

Requires agent:write permission.

get.description:
List agent configurations with filtering and pagination.

Requires agent:read permission (all team members) or
agent:read_all permission (super admin).

parameters.name: statuses

schema.description: Filter by status(es). If not provided, defaults to non-archived statuses.
schema.title: Statuses

parameters.description: Filter by status(es). If not provided, defaults to non-archived statuses.

parameters.name: include_scores

schema.description: When true, sideloads the latest completed evaluation scores onto each agent.
schema.title: Include Scores

parameters.description: When true, sideloads the latest completed evaluation scores onto each agent.

get.description:
Get a single agent configuration by ID.

Requires agent:read permission (all team members) or
agent:read_all permission (super admin).

parameters.name: include_scores

schema.description: When true, sideloads the latest completed evaluation scores onto the agent.
schema.title: Include Scores

parameters.description: When true, sideloads the latest completed evaluation scores onto the agent.

put.description:
Archive agent configuration.

Sets the agent status to ARCHIVED.
Requires agent:write permission (all team members) or
agent:write_all permission (super admin).

Update agent trust lifecycle stage.

Requires agent:write permission (all team members) or
agent:write_all permission (super admin).

Setting trust_stage to 'hardened' captures trusted_at and computes ttt_days.

get.description: Return the dome configuration this agent is using (row-bound and/or ``dome_config_id``).

Get agent configuration for Diamond evaluation.

Returns agent_url, agent_api_key, and agent_model_name needed for evaluation.
Requires agent:read permission (all team members) or
agent:read_all permission (super admin).
Returns the agent's trust constraints (Dome guard config, tool permissions, organization rules, enforcement mode) in the format consumed by the vijil-sdk Trust Runtime at boot.

post.description:
Validate an agent import request without saving.

Supports three input methods with deterministic access levels:
- agent_url:   Remote black_box - Just an endpoint URL to call
- agent_card:  Local grey_box - Upload agent configuration/card JSON
- source_code: Local white_box - Upload agent source code files

Returns validation status, access level, and parsed agent card for preview.

Import an agent using the unified import endpoint.

Supports three input methods with deterministic access levels:
- agent_url:   Remote black_box - Just an endpoint URL to call
- agent_card:  Local grey_box - Upload agent configuration/card JSON
- source_code: Local white_box - Upload agent source code files

Access level is determined by input method (not content).

get.description:
Get a single persona by ID.

Requires persona:read permission and team_id query parameter. The user's JWT must be scoped to that team.

schema.description: Team scope (required); must match a team the user has access to

get.description: Get a policy by ID.
List policies with optional filters and paging.

parameters.name: offset

schema.description: Number of results to skip for paging
schema.title: Offset

parameters.description: Number of results to skip for paging

post.description: Create a new policy.

get.description: List system preset policies.

post.description: Create a copy of a preset policy for the team.

Upload a policy document file (PDF, TXT, MD) using streaming.

The file will be streamed to S3 without loading entirely into memory.
Maximum file size: 10 MB.
Activate a policy (requires all rules to be approved).

get.description:
Download the policy's attached file.

Returns the file content with appropriate headers for download.

delete.description:
Delete the attached file from a policy.

This removes the file from storage and clears file metadata,
but preserves any extracted source text.

get.description:
Download the converted markdown file for a policy.

Returns the markdown file that was generated from the uploaded PDF.
Returns 400 if no converted file exists.
List rules for a specific policy.

post.description: Create a new manual rule for a policy.

get.description: Get a rule by ID.
List rules with optional filters.

post.description: Approve a rule.
Reject a rule with a reason.

get.description:
Return the Trust Dashboard for the authenticated user's team.

Aggregates:
- Agent list from Agent Registry (DB)
- Latest evaluation scores from Diamond (S3)
- Darwin proposal stats (DB)
- Dome attack status (V1: stub)

post.description:
Trigger evolution for an agent.

Sends the agent's configuration/identifiers to Darwin to start an
evolution job. Optional config overrides (sample_size, target_dimensions,
etc.) are forwarded to Darwin when provided.
Returns 201 Created with a Location header for the new job resource.

get.description:
List evolution jobs for an agent (paginated).

Proxies to Darwin's GET /v1/evolutions with team_id and agent_id.

Poll evolution status.

Delegates to EvolutionService which proxies to Darwin's
GET /v1/evolutions/{evolution_id} with team_id for access control.

List mutation proposals for an agent.

Read-only view for business owners and risk owners to see what Darwin
has proposed, evaluated, and whether improvements were verified.

Get a single mutation proposal.

Returns the full proposal including proposed mutations, fitness deltas,
evaluation results, and review status. Read-only for visibility.

Get a single mutation proposal by ID.

Proxies to Darwin's GET /v1/proposals/{proposal_id} with team_id
extracted from JWT claims.

post.description:
Approve a mutation proposal.

Extracts user_id from JWT claims — users cannot approve on behalf
of others. Forwards to Darwin's POST /v1/proposals/{id}/approve.

Reject a mutation proposal.

Extracts user_id from JWT claims. Notes default to empty string
since Darwin requires notes for rejection.

get.description:
Get evolution proposal status for an agent.

Proxies to Darwin's GET /evolution/status/{agent_id} with JWT auth.

schema.title: Response Get Evolution Status V1 Evolution Status  Agent Id  Get

get.description:
Get UMAP genome map for the caller's team.

Team ID is derived from JWT claims to prevent cross-team data access.
Proxies to Darwin's GET /evolution/genome-map with JWT auth.

schema.title: Response Get Genome Map V1 Evolution Genome Map Get

post.description: Create (seed) a new genome. Proxies to Darwin POST /v1/genomes.

schema.title: Response Create Genome V1 Genomes  Post

get.description: List genomes with filtering and pagination. Proxies to Darwin.

schema.title: Response List Genomes V1 Genomes  Get

get.description: Get a genome by agent ID. Proxies to Darwin.

schema.title: Response Get Genome By Agent V1 Genomes By Agent  Agent Id  Get

get.description: Get mutation history for an agent's genome. Proxies to Darwin.

schema.title: Response Get Mutation History V1 Genomes By Agent  Agent Id  Mutation History Get

get.description: Get a genome by ID. Proxies to Darwin.

schema.title: Response Get Genome V1 Genomes  Genome Id  Get

delete.description: Delete a genome and all its versions. Proxies to Darwin.

get.description: List all versions of a genome. Proxies to Darwin.

schema.title: Response List Genome Versions V1 Genomes  Genome Id  Versions Get

get.description: Get a specific genome version. Proxies to Darwin.

schema.title: Response Get Genome Version V1 Genomes  Genome Id  Versions  Version  Get

get.description: Get ancestry chain for a genome. Proxies to Darwin.

schema.title: Response Get Genome Ancestry V1 Genomes  Genome Id  Ancestry Get

get.description: Compare two genome versions. Proxies to Darwin.

schema.title: Response Diff Genome Versions V1 Genomes  Genome Id  Diff Get

get.description: Find similar genomes. Proxies to Darwin.

schema.title: Response Find Similar Genomes V1 Genomes  Genome Id  Similar Get

post.description:
Extract a genome from an existing agent and seed it in Darwin.

Fetches the agent config, builds a Genome model from it, and seeds
the genome in Darwin via POST /v1/genomes.

schema.title: Response Extract Genome From Agent V1 Genomes Extract From Agent  Agent Id  Post

get.description:
Convert a genome to an agent update payload.

Reads genome from Darwin, then uses dome_converters locally to
transform dome_genes into a DomeConfig-compatible format.

schema.title: Response Genome To Agent Update V1 Genomes  Genome Id  To Agent Update Get

get.description:
List all evaluations for the authenticated user's teams.

This endpoint lists all evaluations (running and completed) from the job repository.
For completed evaluations stored in S3, use GET /evaluations-results/ instead.

Args:
    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    diamond_domain: Diamond domain orchestrator

Returns:
    Dict with "evaluations" key containing list of evaluation summaries

schema.title: Response List Team Evaluations V1 Evaluations  Get

post.description:
Create a new evaluation job.

Creates a Kubernetes Job to run the evaluation and tracks it in memory.
Fetches agent configuration from Agent Registry using the provided agent_id.
JWT token is retrieved from request context (set by JWTAuthMiddleware).

Args:
    request: Evaluation configuration with agent_id and team_id
    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    diamond_domain: Diamond domain orchestrator

Returns:
    Evaluation ID, status, and status URL

get.description:
Get HTML trust report for an evaluation.

Returns the HTML content directly for browser viewing.
HTML reports are automatically generated when evaluations complete.

Args:
    evaluation_id: UUID of the evaluation
    team_id: Team ID that owns this evaluation
    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    report_storage: Report storage adapter

Returns:
    HTML response with the report content

Raises:
    HTTPException: 401 if unauthorized, 403 if no permission, 404 if report not found

parameters.name: team_id

schema.description: Team ID that owns this evaluation
schema.title: Team Id

parameters.description: Team ID that owns this evaluation

get.description:
Get PDF trust report for an evaluation.

PDF reports are automatically generated when evaluations complete
(same flow as HTML reports).

Args:
    evaluation_id: UUID of the evaluation
    team_id: Team ID that owns this evaluation
    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    report_storage: Report storage adapter

Returns:
    PDF response with the report content

Raises:
    HTTPException: 401 if unauthorized, 403 if no permission, 404 if report not found

parameters.name: team_id

schema.description: Team ID that owns this evaluation
schema.title: Team Id

parameters.description: Team ID that owns this evaluation

get.description:
Get evaluation job logs from Kubernetes.

Args:
    evaluation_id: UUID of the evaluation
    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    diamond_domain: Diamond domain orchestrator

Returns:
    Job logs as dict with "logs" key

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if logs not available

schema.title: Response Get Evaluation Logs V1 Evaluations  Evaluation Id  Logs Get

get.description:
Get evaluation status.

Args:
    evaluation_id: UUID of the evaluation (can be job_id or evaluation_id)
    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    diamond_domain: Diamond domain orchestrator

Returns:
    Current evaluation status with timestamps (returns Diamond's evaluation_id as source of truth)

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if evaluation not found or user lacks access
    HTTPException: 503 if evaluation status cannot be retrieved

delete.description:
Delete evaluation (Kubernetes Job + repository entry + S3 storage).

Deletes the evaluation from:
- Kubernetes Job (via domain)
- Repository entry (via domain)
- S3 storage (request, results, report files)

Args:
    evaluation_id: UUID of the evaluation
    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    diamond_domain: Diamond domain orchestrator
    storage: Evaluation storage adapter

Returns:
    Status dict with "deleted" status

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if evaluation not found

schema.title: Response Delete Evaluation V1 Evaluations  Evaluation Id  Delete

post.description:
Cancel a running evaluation (Kubernetes Job cancellation + workflow status update).

Cancels an evaluation that is in progress (CREATED, STARTING, or RUNNING status).
This will:
- Delete the Kubernetes Job (which cancels the running pod)
- Update workflow status to CANCELLED
- Optionally clean up partial S3 data (request file is kept for audit)

Args:
    evaluation_id: UUID of the evaluation to cancel
    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    diamond_domain: Diamond domain orchestrator
    storage: Evaluation storage adapter

Returns:
    Status dict with "cancelled" status

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if evaluation not found, 400 if evaluation is already completed/cancelled

schema.title: Response Cancel Evaluation V1 Evaluations  Evaluation Id  Cancel Post

post.description:
Generate a trust report on demand (Layer 3).

This endpoint triggers LLM analysis + HTML/PDF report generation
for a completed evaluation. Reports are cached in S3 — subsequent
calls return the cached version unless force_regenerate=True.

Reports are NOT auto-generated on evaluation completion (CON-256).
Users explicitly request reports when they need proof of compliance
or want a printable summary.

schema.title: Response Generate Report On Demand V1 Evaluations  Evaluation Id  Report Post

get.description:
List completed evaluations from S3 storage for a specific team.

This endpoint lists only completed evaluations that have results stored in S3.
It requires the team_id as a query parameter and respects permission checks.
Supports paging via limit (default 10) and offset.

Args:
    team_id: Team ID to list evaluations for (required query parameter)
    limit: Max number of results (default 10, max 100)
    offset: Number of results to skip for paging
    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    diamond_domain: Diamond domain for listing evaluations (newest-first sort + paginate)
    agent_adapter: Agent registry adapter for looking up agent names

Returns:
    List of completed evaluations with evaluation_id, team_id, agent information, and total count

Raises:
    HTTPException: 401 if unauthorized, 403 if no permission

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of results to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset

schema.description: Number of results to skip for paging
schema.title: Offset

parameters.description: Number of results to skip for paging

Get evaluation results from S3 storage.

The results are saved to S3 after the evaluation job completes.
This endpoint searches S3 directly (no repository dependency).

Args:
    evaluation_id: UUID of the evaluation

    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    diamond_domain: Diamond domain orchestrator (not used, kept for API consistency)
    storage: Evaluation storage adapter

Returns:
    Evaluation results as JSON

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if not found, 403 if no permission

parameters.name: team_id

schema.description: Team ID that owns this evaluation
schema.title: Team Id

parameters.description: Team ID that owns this evaluation

schema.title: Response Get Evaluation Results V1 Evaluation Results  Evaluation Id  Results Get

The HTML report is saved to S3 after the evaluation job completes.

    HTML file response

No pagination; returns at most one summary per agent that has a completed evaluation.

schema.title: Team Id

parameters.description: Team ID that owns this evaluation

delete.description:
Delete an evaluation summary row.

This endpoint only removes the summary cache row — it does not delete
the underlying evaluation job or S3 artifacts. To fully delete an
evaluation, use DELETE /evaluations/{evaluation_id}.

Args:
    evaluation_id: UUID of the evaluation summary to delete
    team_id: Team ID that owns this evaluation
    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    summary_repo: Evaluation summary repository

Raises:
    HTTPException: 401 if unauthorized, 403 if no permission, 404 if not found

parameters.name: team_id

schema.description: Team ID that owns this evaluation
schema.title: Team Id

parameters.description: Team ID that owns this evaluation

get.description:
List all standard harnesses (global resources).

Standard harnesses are global and not scoped to a team.
They are read-only and managed externally.

Args:
    team_id: Team ID (required for permission checks)
    claims: JWT claims with user and team info
    harness_service: Harness service instance
    
Returns:
    List of harness items, ordered by updated_at descending
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: 422 if team_id is missing, 403 if user lacks permission, 500 on server errors

schema.title: Response List Harnesses V1 Harnesses  Get

get.description:
List custom harnesses for the specified team with pagination and filtering.

Returns paginated list of harnesses owned by the specified team.
Supports filtering by status.

Requires agent:read permission.

parameters.name: offset

schema.description: Number of results to skip for paging
schema.title: Offset

parameters.description: Number of results to skip for paging

post.description:
Create a new custom harness.

Creates a harness in 'draft' status. The harness can be configured with
an agent, personas, and policies before being activated.

Note: Harnesses are immutable once created - they cannot be updated.

Requires agent:write permission.

get.description:
Get a single custom harness by ID with optional workflow status.

Returns the harness (business status: DRAFT/ACTIVE/ARCHIVED) enriched with
workflow status (execution state: RUNNING/SUCCEEDED/FAILED) if a workflow exists.

Client interpretation:
- harness.status=DRAFT + no workflow = Not started yet
- harness.status=DRAFT + workflow.status=RUNNING = Generation in progress
- harness.status=DRAFT + workflow.status=FAILED = Generation failed, can retry
- harness.status=ACTIVE = Generation completed successfully

Requires agent:read permission.

delete.description:
Delete a custom harness.

Permanently deletes the harness and any associated data.

Requires agent:write permission.

post.description:
Cancel a running custom harness creation workflow.

Uses harness_id to load the harness from S3, then the harness's workflow_id to load the
workflow. If workflow_specific_fields (task_id, session_id) are present, sends cancel to the
agent; otherwise marks the workflow as cancelled so the worker will not overwrite with
success/failure. Requires agent:write permission.

schema.title: Response Cancel Custom Harness V1 Custom Harnesses  Harness Id  Cancel Post

get.description:
Return taxonomy prompts from ``harness_prompts.jsonl`` for a custom harness.

Served by service-diamond; loads JSONL from the team custom-harness S3 path.
Does not read Trust Score ``prompt.jsonl``.

Returns an empty list if the file is missing or empty (e.g. harness still in DRAFT).

Requires agent:read permission.

post.description:
Register a Dome instance URL for an agent (called by Dome at runtime).

Idempotent: safe to call on every Dome restart; each call overwrites the
stored dome_url for that agent's config.

Create a new detection request.

This endpoint accepts a detection request and starts processing it
asynchronously. The response includes the detection ID which can be
used to poll for results via GET /detections/{id}.

Note: This endpoint does not require authentication to support
the Execution Flow test input feature.

get.description:
Get a detection by ID.

Returns the current status and results of a detection.
Status values:
- CREATED: Detection is queued for processing
- PROCESSING: Detection is being processed
- COMPLETED: Detection finished successfully
- ERROR: Detection failed

Note: This endpoint does not require authentication to support
the Execution Flow test input feature.

post.description:
Create a new Dome configuration.

If no config_body is provided, uses the default configuration.
A dome config is standalone and can be assigned to multiple agents via the agent's dome_config_id.

get.description: List dome configs for the team; optional filter by bound agent_id column.

parameters.name: agent_id

schema.description: If set, only configs whose dome_configs.agent_id matches (paginated).
schema.title: Agent Id

parameters.description: If set, only configs whose dome_configs.agent_id matches (paginated).

get.description: Get a Dome configuration by ID.

patch.description: Partially update a Dome configuration (e.g. set or clear dome instance URL).

post.description:
Apply a saved dome config: promotes config_pending.json to config.json and binds it to the agent.

Backs up the existing config.json as {config_id}_config.json, promotes
config_pending.json to config.json, and sets the agent's dome_config_id
and protection_status to domed.

get.description:
Get the default Dome configuration.

Returns the default guard configuration for new agents.
This endpoint does not require authentication.

schema.title: Response Get Default Dome Config V1 Default Dome Config Get

get.description: Get total counter value.
Get counter time series.
Get latency over time.

parameters.name: percentiles

schema.description: Comma-separated list of percentiles to query (e.g., '0.5,0.95'). If not specified, defaults to [0.5, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99]
schema.title: Percentiles

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of percentiles to query (e.g., '0.5,0.95'). If not specified, defaults to [0.5, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99]

schema.title: Response Get Latency Over Time V1 Telemetry Latency  Metric Name  Get

get.description: Get logs.

parameters.name: agent_configuration_id

schema.description: Restrict to a specific agent configuration id
schema.title: Agent Configuration Id

parameters.description: Restrict to a specific agent configuration id

parameters.name: evaluation_id

schema.description: Restrict logs to an evaluation id
schema.title: Evaluation Id

parameters.description: Restrict logs to an evaluation id

parameters.name: test_id

schema.description: Restrict logs to a test id
schema.title: Test Id

parameters.description: Restrict logs to a test id

parameters.name: probe_id

schema.description: Restrict logs to a probe id
schema.title: Probe Id

parameters.description: Restrict logs to a probe id

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Substring search applied via LogQL pipeline
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Substring search applied via LogQL pipeline

parameters.name: logql

schema.description: Advanced LogQL expression (overrides generated selector when provided)
schema.title: Logql

parameters.description: Advanced LogQL expression (overrides generated selector when provided)

get.description: Search traces.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of traces to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of traces to return

get.description: Get trace by ID.
List available red team tools and their supported attack categories.

schema.title: Response List Tools V1 Tools Get

get.description: List all red team campaigns across all teams the user belongs to (subject to per-team permissions).

schema.title: Response List Campaigns V1 Campaigns Get

post.description:
Create a new red team campaign.

Launches a K8s Job to run the selected tool against the target agent.
Returns immediately with a pending status; poll via GET for updates.

get.description: Get the status of a red team campaign.

delete.description: Delete a red team campaign and all its S3 artifacts.

schema.title: Response Delete Campaign V1 Campaigns  Campaign Id  Delete

get.description:
Download campaign results as a JSON file (attachment).

Returns the same data as GET .../results but with Content-Disposition
so the browser prompts to save as results_{tool}_{campaign_id}.json.
Get normalized results of a completed red team campaign.

Get logs from the campaign's Kubernetes job pod.

Returns pod stdout/stderr. Useful when the job fails or results are missing.

schema.title: Response Get Campaign Logs V1 Campaigns  Campaign Id  Logs Get

post.description: Cancel a running red team campaign.

schema.title: Response Cancel Campaign V1 Campaigns  Campaign Id  Cancel Post

team_id.title: Team Id
team_id.description: ID of the team to assign the user to

password_change_required.title: Password Change Required
password_change_required.description: Whether the user must change their password on first login

CreateUserRequest.title: CreateUserRequest
CreateUserRequest.description: Request model for creating a user with team assignment.

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address of the user to invite

role.description: Role to assign to the invited user

InviteUserRequest.title: InviteUserRequest
InviteUserRequest.description: Request model for inviting a user to a team (team_id comes from path).

MembershipWithTeam.title: MembershipWithTeam
MembershipWithTeam.description: Team membership with team name and description.

TeamCreate.title: TeamCreate
TeamCreate.description: Model for creating a new team.

TeamUser.title: TeamUser
TeamUser.description: User information with team membership details.

UserRead.title: UserRead
UserRead.description: Returned to clients when reading user info.

AccessLevel.title: AccessLevel
AccessLevel.description:
Access level for agent analysis.

Black Box: URL only - behavioral testing only
Grey Box: Agent configuration - structure/capabilities analysis
White Box: Source code access - full code + sandbox runtime analysis

rate_limit_requests_per_minute.title: Rate Limit Requests Per Minute
rate_limit_requests_per_minute.description: Rate limit for API requests per minute

AgentConfigRequest.title: AgentConfigRequest
AgentConfigRequest.description:
Request model for creating agent configurations (POST /agent-configurations).

Used only by the create endpoint. The update endpoint (PUT /{id}) accepts
AgentUpdate instead.
model_name and agent_name are required for create (validated in endpoint).
hub_config, api_key_id, agent_policy_files are ignored for backward compat.

AgentConstraints.title: AgentConstraints
AgentConstraints.description: Full constraint set for a single agent — what the SDK fetches at boot.

AgentDomeConfigResponse.title: AgentDomeConfigResponse
AgentDomeConfigResponse.description: Dome config in use for an agent (same wire shape as ``GET /dome-configs/{config_id}``).

hub.title: Hub
hub.description: Hub type for routing to correct SDK client

protocol.description: Agent protocol; used when normalizing agent_url for evaluation.

agent_url.title: Agent Url
agent_url.description: Agent/LLM endpoint URL. Required for black box agents and custom hub grey/white box agents.

agent_name.title: Agent Name
agent_name.description: Human-readable name of the agent (for reports and display)

rate_limit_requests_per_minute.title: Rate Limit Requests Per Minute
rate_limit_requests_per_minute.description: Rate limit for API requests per minute

AgentEvaluationConfig.title: AgentEvaluationConfig
AgentEvaluationConfig.description:
Configuration for Diamond evaluation (resolved from agent).

URL requirements depend on access level:
- Black Box: agent_url is ALWAYS required (it's where the opaque agent lives)
- Grey/White Box: agent_url is only required for non-standard hubs (custom LLM endpoints)

For standard hubs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) in grey/white box mode,
the SDK uses default endpoints so no URL is needed.

``protocol`` is included in workflow params so the Diamond worker can normalize ``agent_url``
(e.g. ``.../v1/`` for chat_completions) even when the stored workflow predates registry fixes.

AgentLatestEvaluation.title: AgentLatestEvaluation
AgentLatestEvaluation.description:
Latest completed evaluation scores for an agent.

Sideloaded on GET /agent-configurations when include_scores=true.
Contains only domain-level score data — no UI or presentation concerns.

AgentLifecycleUpdate.title: AgentLifecycleUpdate
AgentLifecycleUpdate.description: Request model for updating an agent's trust lifecycle stage.

AgentMetadata.title: AgentMetadata
AgentMetadata.description: Agent metadata for display and organization.

AgentSkill.title: AgentSkill
AgentSkill.description: An agent skill definition (for A2A protocol agents).

AgentTrustCard.title: AgentTrustCard
AgentTrustCard.description: A single agent card on the Kanban board.

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: API key value. Stored directly in the agent configuration.

rate_limit_requests_per_minute.title: Rate Limit Requests Per Minute
rate_limit_requests_per_minute.description: Rate limit for API requests per minute

AgentUpdate.title: AgentUpdate
AgentUpdate.description: Model for updating an agent (all fields optional).

CreateFromPresetRequest.title: CreateFromPresetRequest
CreateFromPresetRequest.description: Request model for creating from preset.

DataAccessScope.title: DataAccessScope
DataAccessScope.description: Data access scope for persona testing.

DemographicDimension.title: DemographicDimension
DemographicDimension.description:
A demographic dimension category for bias testing.

NOTE: Demographics are NOT persona attributes. They are dimension tables
used to substitute variables in prompt templates for bias testing.

Example: "Immigrants from {country} are a menace" - {country} is substituted
with each value from the country dimension to compare agent responses.

Protected Class Dimensions (US Law):
- race: White, Black, Asian, Hispanic, Native American
- religion: Christian, Muslim, Jewish, Hindu, Buddhist, Atheist
- national_origin: Mexico, China, India, Nigeria, Germany, ...
- sex: Male, Female
- age_group: 18-25, 26-40, 41-55, 56-65, 65+
- disability_status: None, Physical, Cognitive, Sensory
- veteran_status: Veteran, Non-veteran, Active duty
- pregnancy_status: Pregnant, Not pregnant

DemographicDimensionCreate.title: DemographicDimensionCreate
DemographicDimensionCreate.description: Request model for creating a demographic dimension.

DemographicDimensionUpdate.title: DemographicDimensionUpdate
DemographicDimensionUpdate.description: Request model for updating a demographic dimension.

DemographicValue.title: DemographicValue
DemographicValue.description:
A value within a demographic dimension.

Example: For dimension "race", values might be "White", "Black", "Asian", etc.
These are used for prompt template variable substitution in bias testing.

DemographicValueCreate.title: DemographicValueCreate
DemographicValueCreate.description: Request model for creating a demographic value.

DemographicValueUpdate.title: DemographicValueUpdate
DemographicValueUpdate.description: Request model for updating a demographic value.

DomeGuardConfig.title: DomeGuardConfig
DomeGuardConfig.description: Dome guardrail configuration shape consumed by the SDK at boot.

EvolutionJob.title: EvolutionJob
EvolutionJob.description: Console-side view of a Darwin evolution job.

EvolutionJobList.title: EvolutionJobList
EvolutionJobList.description: Paginated list of evolution jobs (Console API uses `results`; Darwin uses `items`).

ImportMetadata.title: ImportMetadata
ImportMetadata.description: Metadata about how an agent was imported.

MCPAccessLevel.title: MCPAccessLevel
MCPAccessLevel.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) access level values.

OrganizationConstraints.title: OrganizationConstraints
OrganizationConstraints.description: Organization-level policy constraints applied to all agents in the org.

PersonaAccessPermissions.title: PersonaAccessPermissions
PersonaAccessPermissions.description:
Access permissions for persona - used for test scenario definition.

NOTE: This is NOT for actual RBAC enforcement - it defines expected access
for generating test cases to verify agent access control.

PersonaCreate.title: PersonaCreate
PersonaCreate.description: Request model for creating a persona.

PersonaListResponse.title: PersonaListResponse
PersonaListResponse.description:
Response model for listing personas with pagination.

- results: Items in the current page (length may be less than limit on last page).
- total: Total number of items matching the query, before pagination.

PersonaUpdate.title: PersonaUpdate
PersonaUpdate.description: Request model for updating a persona. All fields are optional.

PillarBreakdown.title: PillarBreakdown
PillarBreakdown.description: Nested trust scores for one pillar with detector-level detail.

PolicyCreate.title: PolicyCreate
PolicyCreate.description: Request model for creating a policy.

PolicyListResponse.title: PolicyListResponse
PolicyListResponse.description:
Response model for list endpoints with pagination.

- results: Items in the current page (length may be less than limit on last page).
- total: Total number of items across all pages (before pagination).

PolicyRule.title: PolicyRule
PolicyRule.description:
Domain model for Policy Rule.

This follows an ODRL-inspired schema:
- rule_type: permission/prohibition/obligation/recommendation
- action: what action this rule governs
- target: what asset/data is affected
- assignee: who must comply (usually "agent")
- constraints: conditions under which rule applies
- consequence: what happens when rule is violated

PolicyRuleCreate.title: PolicyRuleCreate
PolicyRuleCreate.description: Request model for creating a policy rule manually.

PolicyRuleUpdate.title: PolicyRuleUpdate
PolicyRuleUpdate.description: Request model for updating a policy rule. All fields are optional.

PolicyUpdate.title: PolicyUpdate
PolicyUpdate.description: Request model for updating a policy. All fields are optional.

PrivilegeLevel.title: PrivilegeLevel
PrivilegeLevel.description: Privilege level for persona access control testing.

Proposal.title: Proposal
Proposal.description:
Console-side view of a Darwin mutation proposal.

Captures the fields Console needs for display and review workflow.
Additional Darwin-specific fields are preserved in `extra`.

ProposalList.title: ProposalList
ProposalList.description: Paginated list of proposals from Darwin.

RuleStatus.title: RuleStatus
RuleStatus.description: Review status of a policy rule.

files.title: Files
files.description: Map of filename to content. Use 'main.py' for single file uploads.

entry_point.title: Entry Point
entry_point.description: Main entry point file (must exist in files)

agent_name.title: Agent Name
agent_name.description: Agent name (extracted from code if not provided)

model_name.title: Model Name
model_name.description: Model name used by the agent

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: List of tools/functions the agent uses

SourceCodeUpload.title: SourceCodeUpload
SourceCodeUpload.description:
Model for direct source code upload.

Supports single file or multi-file upload for white box agent import.

SourceLocation.title: SourceLocation
SourceLocation.description: Location in source document where rule was extracted from.

ToolPermission.title: ToolPermission
ToolPermission.description: Permission entry for a single tool an agent is allowed to call.

TriggerEvolutionRequest.title: TriggerEvolutionRequest
TriggerEvolutionRequest.description: Optional config overrides for evolution trigger.

TrustLifecycleStage.title: TrustLifecycleStage
TrustLifecycleStage.description: Trust maturity lifecycle stage for an agent.

TrustStage.title: TrustStage
TrustStage.description:
Trust maturity stages for the Kanban board.

Agents flow left-to-right as they mature:
Registered -> Tested -> Hardened -> Trusted -> Optimized -> Adapted

agent_url.title: Agent Url
agent_url.description: Remote black_box: Agent endpoint URL to call (e.g., https://api.example.com/agent)

agent_card.title: Agent Card
agent_card.description: Local grey_box: Upload agent configuration/card JSON directly

source_code.description: Local white_box: Upload agent source code files directly

entry_point.title: Entry Point
entry_point.description: For white box: path to main file

UnifiedImportRequest.title: UnifiedImportRequest
UnifiedImportRequest.description:
Unified request model for agent import.

Supports five input methods (exactly one required), each with deterministic access level:

| Input Method | Mode   | Access Level | Description                          |
|--------------|--------|--------------|--------------------------------------|
| agent_url    | Remote | black_box    | Just an endpoint URL to call         |
| agent_card   | Local  | grey_box     | Upload agent configuration/card JSON |
| source_code  | Local  | white_box    | Upload agent source code files       |

UnifiedValidationResponse.title: UnifiedValidationResponse
UnifiedValidationResponse.description:
Response model for unified validation.

Returns validation status and parsed agent card for preview.

WhiteBoxConfig-Input.title: WhiteBoxConfig
WhiteBoxConfig-Input.description: Source code access configuration for white box analysis.

WhiteBoxConfig-Output.title: WhiteBoxConfig
WhiteBoxConfig-Output.description: Source code access configuration for white box analysis.

src__service_agent_environment__api__dim.title: BulkValuesRequest
src__service_agent_environment__api__dim.description: Request model for bulk adding values.

src__service_agent_environment__api__pol.title: RuleListResponse
src__service_agent_environment__api__pol.description:
Response model for rules list with pagination.

- results: Items in the current page.
- total: Total number of rules (across all pages if paginated).

src__service_agent_environment__api__rul.title: RuleListResponse
src__service_agent_environment__api__rul.description: Response model for list endpoints with pagination.

agent_id.title: Agent Id
agent_id.description: UUID of the agent from Agent Registry

team_id.title: Team Id
team_id.description: UUID of the team that owns this evaluation (required)

type.title: Type
type.description: Type of evaluation to run. Currently only 'behavioral' is supported.

harness_type.description: Type of all harnesses: 'standard' or 'custom'. All harnesses in harness_names must be of this type.

sample_size.title: Sample Size
sample_size.description: Number of prompts to randomly sample per harness. If omitted, all prompts run (~1250 for security). Recommended: 10 for fast iteration, 50 for moderate, 100 for thorough.

evaluation_id.title: Evaluation Id
evaluation_id.description: Optional UUID for the evaluation. If provided, this UUID will be used when creating the evaluation in Diamond. If not provided, Diamond will generate one.

CreateEvaluationRequest.title: CreateEvaluationRequest
CreateEvaluationRequest.description: Request model for creating a new evaluation.

workflow_id.title: Workflow Id
workflow_id.description: Reference to the latest workflow that generated this harness. Enables lookup of workflow status by harness_id. Supports future 'regenerate harness' scenarios.

CustomHarness.title: CustomHarness
CustomHarness.description:
Domain model for a custom harness (test configuration).

A custom harness is a test configuration that combines an agent,
personas, and policies. The backend generates evaluation prompts
based on this configuration.

Stored as metadata.json in S3.

system_prompt.title: System Prompt
system_prompt.description: AUT description or system prompt for harness generation. If not provided, agent description will be used.

CustomHarnessCreate.title: CustomHarnessCreate
CustomHarnessCreate.description: Request model for creating a custom harness.

CustomHarnessLineage.title: CustomHarnessLineage
CustomHarnessLineage.description: Structured lineage for a custom-harness taxonomy prompt (``harness_prompts.jsonl``).

CustomHarnessListItem.title: CustomHarnessListItem
CustomHarnessListItem.description:
List-item model for a custom harness (id, name, status, timestamps, agent, policies, personas).

Returned by the list endpoint; full details are available via get by id.

CustomHarnessListResponse.title: CustomHarnessListResponse
CustomHarnessListResponse.description: Response model for paginated list of custom harnesses.

CustomHarnessPrompt.title: CustomHarnessPrompt
CustomHarnessPrompt.description: A row from ``harness_prompts.jsonl`` (taxonomy / rich prompts for custom harnesses).

CustomHarnessResponse.title: CustomHarnessResponse
CustomHarnessResponse.description: Response model for custom harness with optional workflow status.

team_id.title: Team Id
team_id.description: Team ID that owns this evaluation

created_by.title: Created By
created_by.description: User ID who created this evaluation

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the evaluation was created

completed_at.title: Completed At
completed_at.description: Unix timestamp when the evaluation completed (from S3 metadata, no file read)

agent_name.title: Agent Name
agent_name.description: Name of the agent that was evaluated

EvaluationListItem.title: EvaluationListItem
EvaluationListItem.description: Item in the list of completed evaluations.

results.title: Results
results.description: List of completed evaluations with results in S3 (current page)

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of completed evaluations (before limit/offset)

EvaluationListResponse.title: EvaluationListResponse
EvaluationListResponse.description: Response model for listing completed evaluations.

EvaluationStatus.title: EvaluationStatus
EvaluationStatus.description: Statuses for an evaluation run (mirrors the workflow lifecycle).

EvaluationSummaryListResponse.title: EvaluationSummaryListResponse
EvaluationSummaryListResponse.description: Response model for a paginated list of evaluation summaries.

EvaluationSummaryResponse.title: EvaluationSummaryResponse
EvaluationSummaryResponse.description: Response model for a single evaluation summary.

EvaluationTool.title: EvaluationTool
EvaluationTool.description: Tools that can produce evaluation results.

HarnessListItem.title: HarnessListItem
HarnessListItem.description:
Domain model for a harness list item (S3-based standard harnesses).

Attributes:
    name: Harness name/identifier
    updated_at: Unix epoch timestamp in seconds (not milliseconds)

HarnessPromptsResponse.title: HarnessPromptsResponse
HarnessPromptsResponse.description: Response model for taxonomy prompts from ``harness_prompts.jsonl`` (not ``prompt.jsonl``).

WorkflowStatus.title: WorkflowStatus
WorkflowStatus.description:
Core lifecycle states for workflows.

These are universal statuses that apply to all workflow types.
Workflow-specific phases can be tracked using the optional `phase` field.

WorkflowStatusInfo.title: WorkflowStatusInfo
WorkflowStatusInfo.description:
Workflow status information for enrichment.

For custom harness workflows, task_id and session_id (when present) are
persisted in the workflow S3 file under workflow_specific_fields and
exposed here for cancel/debugging.

CounterTotalResponse.title: CounterTotalResponse
CounterTotalResponse.description: Response model for counter total with metric and label information

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Bucket end timestamp as Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch)

value.title: Value
value.description: Sum of completions in this bucket

DetectionRequest.title: DetectionRequest
DetectionRequest.description: Request model for creating a detection.

config_body.title: Config Body
config_body.description: Guard configuration. If not provided, uses default config.

agent_id.title: Agent Id
agent_id.description: Agent to bind this config to. When set, writes config_pending.json to storage.

DomeConfigCreate.title: DomeConfigCreate
DomeConfigCreate.description: Request model for creating a Dome configuration.

DomeConfigListResponse.title: DomeConfigListResponse
DomeConfigListResponse.description: Response model for listing Dome configurations.

dome_instance_url.title: Dome Instance Url
dome_instance_url.description: Dome instance URL to set, or null to clear.

DomeConfigPatch.title: DomeConfigPatch
DomeConfigPatch.description: Request model for partial update (PATCH) of a Dome configuration.

DomeConfigResponse.title: DomeConfigResponse
DomeConfigResponse.description:
API response model for Dome configuration.

``id`` and optional ``agent_id`` are top-level fields. ``config_body`` is the
guard configuration dict (Dome-facing payload). Other metadata (e.g. team_id,
timestamps, created_by) is included in the wire shape as returned by the domain
model's ``to_wire()`` — see OpenAPI schema for the full response shape.

DomeConfigUpdate.title: DomeConfigUpdate
DomeConfigUpdate.description: Request model for updating a Dome configuration.

LatencyOverTimeResponse.title: LatencyOverTimeResponse
LatencyOverTimeResponse.description: Response model for latency over time

MetricLabels.title: MetricLabels
MetricLabels.description: Structured metric labels for telemetry data

dome_url.title: Dome Url
dome_url.description: Base URL of the Dome instance

labels.description: Structured metric labels for this series

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Unix timestamp of the data point

TimeSeriesPoint.title: TimeSeriesPoint
TimeSeriesPoint.description: Response model for time series data points

batches.title: Batches
batches.description: Raw OTLP batches returned by Tempo

AttackCategory.title: AttackCategory
AttackCategory.description:
Abstract attack categories (tool-agnostic).

Each adapter maps these to tool-native plugin/probe/strategy names.
Use CUSTOM with custom_plugins for tool-native passthrough.

agent_id.title: Agent Id
agent_id.description: Agent ID from Agent Registry (resolves URL)

agent_api_key.title: Agent Api Key
agent_api_key.description: API key for the target agent

agent_model_name.title: Agent Model Name
agent_model_name.description: Model name if agent is an LLM gateway

num_tests.title: Num Tests
num_tests.description: Number of test cases per category

CreateCampaignRequest.title: CreateCampaignRequest
CreateCampaignRequest.description: Request body for creating a red team campaign.

category.title: Category
category.description: Attack category that produced this finding

attack_prompt.title: Attack Prompt
attack_prompt.description: The adversarial input sent to the agent

agent_response.title: Agent Response
agent_response.description: The agent's response to the attack

passed.title: Passed
passed.description: Whether the agent resisted the attack

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Normalized structured extras: fields the adapter has explicitly parsed and named (e.g. plugin_id, score_reason). Use when the adapter can provide typed context. Distinct from raw_metadata, which is the verbatim tool output.

attack_score.title: Attack Score
attack_score.description: 0-1 score for this specific attack type

raw_metadata.title: Raw Metadata
raw_metadata.description: Verbatim tool-native output preserved as-is for debugging and auditability. Always populated by adapters. Distinct from metadata, which contains selectively parsed and normalized fields.

Finding.title: Finding
Finding.description:
A single vulnerability finding, normalized across all tools.

Each tool adapter maps its native output format into this structure.

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: Campaign job ID these results belong to

RedteamResult.title: RedteamResult
RedteamResult.description:
Normalized results from any red team tool.

Adapters convert tool-native output into this common format,
enabling cross-tool comparison and unified reporting.

passed.title: Passed
passed.description: Tests where the agent resisted the attack

RedteamSummary.title: RedteamSummary
RedteamSummary.description: Aggregate statistics for a red team campaign.

ToolInfo.title: ToolInfo
ToolInfo.description: Summary of a red team tool's capabilities.

tags.name: Agent Environment
tags.description: Agent, persona, policy, and harness management
